The Phone You Carry
Is Made by the Entire World

3 perspectives to understand how global smartphone supply chains work & shift

↓Swipe

Who Makes Your Smartphone?

Tap a part to trace its origin

No single nation can build a smartphone alone.
Global division of labor penetrates every corner.
Source: apple.com

One Smartphone, Global Collaboration

Win-win cooperation for all

A total of 30 to 35 countries participate in the full industrial chain, among which 10 are core key producers.
15 to 20 countries supply mineral resources and basic components, while the rest only engage in simple supporting links.
Source: Counterpoint, IDC, Digitimes, CAICT, UN Comtrade, and public supply chain disclosures by Apple, Samsung and other enterprises

How a Smartphone Is Made

Swipe or tap arrows to explore each step

✏️
STEP 01 · UPSTREAM · HIGH MARGIN
Product Design
Key players:
Apple · Huawei · Xiaomi · Samsung
Whoever controls design controls pricing & product positioning. The most profitable segment of the entire supply chain.
→
🔬
STEP 02 · MIDSTREAM · CORE
Component Manufacturing
Key players:
TSMC · Samsung Display · Sony · BOE · CATL
Depends on specialized firms across countries — the deepest point of global interdependence. No single nation can be self-sufficient.
→
🏭
STEP 03 · DOWNSTREAM · LABOR-INTENSIVE
Final Assembly
Key players:
Foxconn · Luxshare · Wistron · Compal
Lowest technical barrier — manual labor & assembly lines. Also the segment seeing the most visible global relocation.
→
🌍
STEP 04 · DISTRIBUTION · CLOSED LOOP
Global Sales & Service
Channels:
Brand stores · Carriers · Cross-border e-commerce
Brands control sales channels & capture terminal brand premiums — completing the final loop of the value chain.
← Swipe to explore →
🌐

One Smartphone Reveals the
Truth Behind Global Division

1
No single country can build a smartphone alone. Globalized division of labor is an inevitable trend for electronic products.
2
Industry transfer only relocates low-end assembly. Core components & industrial support ecosystems remain anchored at home.
3
From design → components → assembly → sales: supply chain bargaining power determines real industry profits.
